text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE

Patient Name: Margaret Delacroix
MRN: [REDACTED]
Date of Procedure: [REDACTED]
Report Date: [REDACTED]
Pathologist: [REDACTED]
Surgeon: [REDACTED]
Service: Gynecologic Oncology
Clinical History: 52-year-old postmenopausal woman with biopsy-confirmed high-grade serous carcinoma of suspected ovarian/fallopian tube origin, FIGO stage IIIC, germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (5382insC). Presenting for primary cytoreductive surgery.

---

SPECIMEN ACCESSION NUMBERS AND DESIGNATIONS

Part A: Right fallopian tube and right ovary
Part B: Left fallopian tube and left ovary
Part C: Uterus and cervix with bilateral parametria
Part D: Infracolic omentum
Part E: Appendix
Part F: Right pelvic peritoneum (excision)
Part G: Left pelvic peritoneum (excision)
Part H: Right diaphragmatic peritoneum (strip)
Part I: Left diaphragmatic peritoneum (strip)
Part J: Pelvic lymph nodes (bilateral pelvic dissection)
Part K: Para-aortic lymph nodes (bilateral infrarenal dissection)
Part L: Liver surface implant (excision)

---

GROSS DESCRIPTION

Part A — Right fallopian tube and right ovary: Received fresh in a labeled container. The right fallopian tube measures 7.5 cm in length and 0.8 cm in diameter. The fimbriated end is distorted by a 0.6 cm papillary excrescence with firm, granular texture. The right ovary is enlarged, measuring 8.1 x 6.2 x 4.9 cm, and is replaced by a multiloculated cystic and solid mass. The external surface shows multiple gray-white papillary excrescences and areas of surface involvement. On sectioning, the mass contains thick, hemorrhagic fluid, solid tan-white areas with geographic necrosis, and calcified foci (grossly suggestive of psammoma bodies). Representative sections in 12 cassettes.

Part B — Left fallopian tube and left ovary: The left fallopian tube measures 6.8 cm in length. The fimbriated end contains a 0.4 cm area of mucosal roughening. The left ovary is enlarged, measuring 5.9 x 4.1 x 3.6 cm, with a bosselated external surface and firm, gray-white solid areas. On sectioning, solid and cystic areas noted with focal necrosis. Representative sections in 8 cassettes.

Part C — Uterus and cervix: The uterus with attached cervix and bilateral parametria weighs 98 grams and measures 8.5 x 5.2 x 3.8 cm. The serosal surface is smooth and glistening with no tumor deposits identified grossly. The endometrium is atrophic, measuring 0.2 cm in maximum thickness. The myometrium is heterogeneous with multiple well-defined tan-white intramural nodules ranging from 0.4 to 1.8 cm (consistent with leiomyomata). No endometrial or myometrial lesions grossly suspicious for carcinoma. The cervix is smooth and unremarkable. Representative sections in 8 cassettes.

Part D — Infracolic omentum: Received as a large, fibrofatty specimen measuring 32 x 18 x 3.5 cm in aggregate. The cut surface shows diffuse replacement of omental fat by firm, tan-white to gray, infiltrative tumor tissue with a glistening cut surface in areas. Aggregate tumor mass measures approximately 11.5 x 7.8 x 2.9 cm. Margins are inked. Sections in 10 cassettes including margins.

Part E — Appendix: The vermiform appendix measures 5.8 cm in length and 0.7 cm in diameter. The serosal surface is smooth and tan. On cross-section, the lumen is patent and the wall is unremarkable. No mucocele or intraluminal mass. Representative sections in 2 cassettes.

Part F — Right pelvic peritoneum: Multiple irregular gray-white fragments of fibrofatty peritoneum, in aggregate measuring 9.5 x 7.0 x 1.0 cm. Focal areas of nodular thickening are present. Representative sections in 4 cassettes.

Part G — Left pelvic peritoneum: Multiple peritoneal fragments in aggregate measuring 7.8 x 5.5 x 0.8 cm with focal nodularity. Representative sections in 3 cassettes.

Part H — Right diaphragmatic peritoneum: Multiple thin peritoneal strips, in aggregate 14 x 8 cm, with scattered gray-white nodular deposits ranging from 0.2 to 0.9 cm. All deposits submitted en face in 4 cassettes.

Part I — Left diaphragmatic peritoneum: Peritoneal strips in aggregate 11 x 6.5 cm with focal nodularity (deposits up to 0.5 cm). Representative sections in 3 cassettes.

Part J — Pelvic lymph nodes: Fibrofatty tissue submitted from right external iliac, left external iliac, right internal iliac, left internal iliac, right obturator, and left obturator nodal stations. In aggregate 22 lymph nodes identified, ranging from 0.3 to 2.1 cm in greatest dimension. All nodes submitted in 10 cassettes.

Part K — Para-aortic lymph nodes: Fibrofatty tissue from right and left infrarenal para-aortic regions. In aggregate 14 lymph nodes identified, ranging from 0.4 to 1.4 cm in greatest dimension. All nodes submitted in 6 cassettes.

Part L — Liver surface implant: A single shave of liver surface with adherent capsular tissue and attached tan-white nodule measuring 0.6 x 0.5 x 0.4 cm. The nodule is firm and gray-white on sectioning. Entirely submitted in 1 cassette.

---

MICROSCOPIC FINDINGS

Part A — Right fallopian tube and right ovary:
Fallopian tube: The fimbriated end demonstrates a discrete focus of serous tubal intraepithelial carcinoma (STIC). The STIC lesion is characterized by stratified epithelial cells with high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratios, loss of normal ciliated cell architecture, nuclear pleomorphism, prominent nucleoli, and increased mitotic activity. Immunohistochemical staining confirms: p53 aberrant (diffuse strong overexpression pattern in STIC cells, consistent with TP53 missense mutation; wildtype background tubal epithelium shows heterogeneous weak staining), Ki-67 markedly elevated in STIC cells (~80%). The proximal tube is uninvolved. Serosal surface of tube involved by carcinoma.
Right ovary: Ovarian cortex and surface are diffusely replaced by high-grade serous carcinoma. Characteristic features include: complex papillary and micropapillary architecture, slit-like glandular spaces lined by markedly atypical cells with high nuclear grade (nuclear atypia, prominent nucleoli, macronucleoli), psammoma bodies present (moderate number), geographic areas of tumor necrosis, and a brisk mitotic rate exceeding 15 mitoses per 10 high-power fields. Lymphovascular invasion is identified.

Part B — Left fallopian tube and left ovary:
Fallopian tube: A second STIC lesion is identified in the fimbriated end of the left fallopian tube, morphologically identical to that in the right tube. p53 aberrant immunostaining confirmed. This bilateral STIC involvement strongly supports fallopian tube as the site of origin.
Left ovary: High-grade serous carcinoma involving ovarian cortex and surface, morphologically identical to the right ovary. Extensive surface involvement. No uninvolved cortical tissue identified in sampled sections.

Part C — Uterus and cervix:
Endometrium: Atrophic endometrium, no atypia, no carcinoma.
Myometrium: Three leiomyomata are confirmed microscopically (0.4 cm, 1.2 cm, and 1.8 cm), composed of bland interlacing smooth muscle bundles. No malignant transformation. No carcinoma in the myometrium.
Cervix: Unremarkable squamous and endocervical mucosa. No carcinoma. No high-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion.
Parametria: No carcinoma.

Part D — Infracolic omentum:
The omentum is extensively replaced by high-grade serous carcinoma with a desmoplastic stromal response. Tumor is arranged in nests, papillary clusters, and solid sheets. Extensive tumor necrosis present. Psammoma bodies identified throughout. Lymphovascular spaces within omental fat contain tumor emboli. Surgical excision margins are negative for carcinoma.

Part E — Appendix:
The appendix shows no evidence of carcinoma. Mild periappendiceal adipose tissue fibrosis present. No appendiceal mucinous neoplasm. Serosal surface is uninvolved.

Part F — Right pelvic peritoneum:
Multiple foci of high-grade serous carcinoma are present on the peritoneal surface and within subperitoneal fibrous tissue. Tumor nests with papillary architecture, psammoma bodies, and high-grade nuclear features. Surgical resection margins are negative.

Part G — Left pelvic peritoneum:
High-grade serous carcinoma involving peritoneal surface, morphologically similar to right side. Margins negative.

Part H — Right diaphragmatic peritoneum:
Seven discrete foci of high-grade serous carcinoma are identified on the diaphragmatic peritoneal surface, ranging from 0.1 to 0.9 cm. The largest deposit shows central necrosis. All deposits appear completely excised within the submitted specimen. No involvement of underlying diaphragmatic muscle fibers.

Part I — Left diaphragmatic peritoneum:
Three foci of high-grade serous carcinoma on peritoneal surface, up to 0.5 cm. Completely excised.

Part J — Pelvic lymph nodes:
Metastatic high-grade serous carcinoma identified in 4 of 22 pelvic lymph nodes:
  - Right external iliac: 1/4 positive (largest deposit 1.3 cm, with extranodal extension into perinodal fibroadipose tissue)
  - Left external iliac: 1/4 positive (0.6 cm deposit)
  - Right obturator: 1/4 positive (0.4 cm deposit)
  - Left internal iliac: 1/5 positive (0.5 cm deposit)
  - Right internal iliac: 0/3 negative
  - Left obturator: 0/2 negative
The 18 remaining lymph nodes show no evidence of carcinoma (sinus histiocytosis noted in several reactive nodes).

Part K — Para-aortic lymph nodes:
Metastatic high-grade serous carcinoma in 2 of 14 para-aortic lymph nodes:
  - Right para-aortic: 1/7 positive (0.8 cm, no extranodal extension)
  - Left para-aortic: 1/7 positive (0.5 cm)
The 12 remaining nodes are negative.

Part L — Liver surface implant:
A single 0.6 cm focus of high-grade serous carcinoma involving the liver capsule (Glisson's capsule) with superficial fibrous tissue involvement. No invasion of hepatic parenchyma. This is consistent with a peritoneal implant on the liver surface (Glisson's capsule deposit), not a parenchymal metastasis. Surgical excision complete.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES

Immunohistochemistry (performed on representative sections from Part A, confirmed on Part D):
- WT1: Positive (diffuse, strong nuclear staining in tumor cells)
- PAX8: Positive (nuclear, diffuse)
- CK7: Positive (diffuse, strong)
- CK20: Negative
- Estrogen receptor (ER): Positive (approximately 25-30% of tumor nuclei, moderate intensity)
- Progesterone receptor (PR): Negative
- p53: Aberrant pattern — diffuse strong overexpression in the vast majority of tumor cells (>95%), consistent with TP53 gain-of-function missense mutation (as opposed to null pattern); normal heterogeneous staining in benign stromal cells serves as internal positive control
- Ki-67: Approximately 70-75% proliferative index in high-grade tumor areas
- p16: Diffuse strong block positivity (non-specific in HGSC)
- BRCA1 protein (by IHC): Loss of nuclear expression in tumor cells (staining retained in tumor-associated stromal cells, serving as internal control) — consistent with biallelic BRCA1 inactivation
- BerEP4: Positive
- Calretinin: Negative (excludes mesothelioma)
- MOC31: Positive

Note: The IHC profile (WT1+, PAX8+, CK7+, CK20-, ER+, p53 aberrant) is characteristic of high-grade serous carcinoma of Mullerian origin.

Note on STIC confirmation: Both right and left fallopian tube STIC lesions show the p53-signature pattern with aberrant overexpression, identical to the invasive carcinoma component, supporting clonal origin from the tubal epithelium.

Molecular/Ancillary note: Somatic tumor tissue submitted to molecular pathology for next-generation sequencing per institutional protocol for gynecologic malignancies (results reported separately under molecular pathology accession).

---

DIAGNOSIS

Part A — RIGHT FALLOPIAN TUBE AND RIGHT OVARY:
1. Right fallopian tube: SEROUS TUBAL INTRAEPITHELIAL CARCINOMA (STIC), fimbriated end; p53 aberrant; serosal surface involved by carcinoma.
2. Right ovary: HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA, 8.1 cm, with involvement of ovarian cortex and surface; lymphovascular invasion present.

Part B — LEFT FALLOPIAN TUBE AND LEFT OVARY:
1. Left fallopian tube: SEROUS TUBAL INTRAEPITHELIAL CARCINOMA (STIC), fimbriated end; p53 aberrant.
2. Left ovary: HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA, 5.9 cm, with extensive surface involvement.

Part C — UTERUS AND CERVIX:
- Atrophic endometrium; no carcinoma.
- Uterine leiomyomata, multiple (up to 1.8 cm).
- Cervix: no carcinoma, no dysplasia.
- Parametria: no carcinoma.

Part D — INFRACOLIC OMENTUM:
- HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA, extensive involvement (omental cake 11.5 x 7.8 cm); margins negative.

Part E — APPENDIX:
- No carcinoma. No appendiceal neoplasm.

Part F — RIGHT PELVIC PERITONEUM:
- HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA, multiple foci; margins negative.

Part G — LEFT PELVIC PERITONEUM:
- HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA; margins negative.

Part H — RIGHT DIAPHRAGMATIC PERITONEUM:
- HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA, 7 foci (up to 0.9 cm); completely excised.

Part I — LEFT DIAPHRAGMATIC PERITONEUM:
- HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA, 3 foci (up to 0.5 cm); completely excised.

Part J — PELVIC LYMPH NODES (22 nodes examined):
- METASTATIC HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA, 4/22 lymph nodes positive; largest metastatic deposit 1.3 cm; extranodal extension present in one node (right external iliac station).

Part K — PARA-AORTIC LYMPH NODES (14 nodes examined):
- METASTATIC HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA, 2/14 lymph nodes positive; largest deposit 0.8 cm; no extranodal extension.

Part L — LIVER SURFACE (GLISSON'S CAPSULE) IMPLANT:
- HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA, single focus, 0.6 cm, involving hepatic capsule only; no parenchymal invasion; completely excised.

---

PATHOLOGIC STAGING (AJCC 8th Edition / FIGO 2014)

Primary Tumor (pT): pT3c — Peritoneal metastases beyond pelvis, >2 cm in greatest dimension, including involvement of omentum, diaphragmatic peritoneum, and liver surface capsule (Glisson's capsule implant WITHOUT parenchymal involvement remains pT3c/FIGO IIIC)
Regional Lymph Nodes (pN): pN1 — Retroperitoneal lymph node metastases (pelvic and para-aortic nodes involved; pelvic node largest deposit 1.3 cm)
Distant Metastasis (pM): Not applicable (liver surface/capsular implant classified as peritoneal disease, not parenchymal; pM0 by AJCC)

FINAL PATHOLOGIC STAGE: pT3c pN1 M0 — FIGO Stage IIIC

Cytoreduction Status: R0 — No macroscopic residual disease. All submitted specimens with negative margins. No gross residual carcinoma identified at completion of surgery per operative and pathologic assessment.

Comment: Bilateral STIC lesions with identical p53 aberrant immunophenotype to invasive carcinoma strongly support origin from fallopian tube epithelium. The overall morphologic, immunohistochemical, and molecular profile is fully consistent with HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA of fallopian tube/ovarian origin. The liver surface deposit represents a capsular/peritoneal implant without hepatic parenchymal invasion, and is therefore classified as peritoneal disease (consistent with pT3c/FIGO IIIC, not FIGO Stage IV). Lymphovascular invasion is identified and extranodal extension is present in the right external iliac nodal station.

---

Electronic signature: [ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST, M.D.]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
[INSTITUTION REDACTED]

Dictated and reviewed. Amended if noted.

CC: Gynecologic Oncology Service
